title: Small Business and Start-ups - Green & Spiegel

## Small Business and Start-ups
Home > U.S Immigration-2 > U.S. Immigration > Business Immigration > Small Business and Start-ups

## Helping your business succeed
Immigration procedures for new and small businesses can be especially challenging. Smaller companies do not have the same legal resources as larger entities. Immigration petitions and applications filed by new businesses are treated with great scrutiny – as a matter of law and in day-to-day adjudications – compared to those filed by their larger counterparts.
Based on our institutional experience and the needs of our small business clientele, Green and Spiegel U.S. has developed a different approach than most other multinational law firms. We take a far more personal tact – we endeavor to understand your business fully and your specific needs as a start-up entrepreneur or small business owner/manager. Based on our experience representing hundreds of companies like yours, we are able to craft proactive immigration solutions that are both cost-effective and efficient for both your enterprise and your employees.
In addition to putting in place strong immigration policies at your company from the very beginning, it is equally important to ensure that key foreign nationals are able to work legally for your company in the United States. Although larger companies might more frequently find themselves in the crosshairs of a government compliance audit, it is never too early to reduce your company’s risk.
Whether it is creating a long-term immigration policy, helping with document maintenance or processing visas for key employees, Green and Spiegel U.S. is the perfect firm to keep you focused on what matters most – your business. Our attorneys remain available to provide you with the answers you need to your questions. With all you have to worry about in launching and operating a small business, our goal is to make any intersections with the immigration law as painless as possible.
